title: festuca nevadensis subsp. scabrescens
intro: Festuca nevadensis subsp. scabrescens, also known as the Nevada fescue, is a subspecies of grass native to the mountainous regions of North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system, making it a valuable natural remedy for colds, fever, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Festuca nevadensis subsp. scabrescens can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to soothe sore throats, calm coughs, and provide relief from minor infections.
